experiences.intentions: The active piece is O at the top center, already in its only orientation. The stack is low-to-mid and uneven, with a notable central-left cavity above the lower structures; immediate threat is low because there is plenty of vertical space. Since O cannot benefit from rotation, the useful candidates are left/right/drop. Moving left from columns 4-5 toward columns 3-4 better aligns the O to fill the shallow gap over the left side of the stack and avoids piling more on the tall central-right area near the existing I/Z/T structure. A single left shift is the best setup step before future drops; right would worsen the bumpiness, and dropping immediately at current x is less flexible.

experiences.intentions: Active T is at the very top with a mostly open field above several uneven stacks. The nearest important opportunity is the shallow center-left valley around columns 3-5 above the O/Z/I/J/S/L structure; placing the T flatter is generally more flexible than keeping it upright. My goal now is to orient the T for a clean descent into that central contour while preserving future placements for upcoming L, I, J, S. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would waste the piece high on the open surface; soft_drop commits before alignment; left/right alone repositions but keeps a less useful spawn orientation. Rotating right changes the T from flat-top spawn to a right-facing orientation, a useful intermediate toward the flat-down orientation and improves maneuverability while still high enough to move afterward. So the best single move is to rotate now.
